title: Fermented Velvety Ukrainian Mushroom Dumplings
description: A tangy, silky mushroom filling wrapped in egg roll wrappers and lightly blanched, perfect for a quick hangover cure.
instructions: In a bowl, combine sliced mushrooms, chopped onion, minced garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Add kefir to the mushroom mixture, stir to coat evenly, cover, and refrigerate for 12 hours to allow fermentation and flavor development.
After fermentation, drain excess liquid and let the mixture cool to room temperature.
Place a teaspoon of the mushroom filling in the center of an egg roll wrapper, fold into a triangle, and seal the edges with a dab of water.
Repeat until all wrappers are filled.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and blanch the dumplings for 4–5 minutes until they float and become tender.
Remove with a slotted spoon and pat dry.
In a skillet, melt butter over medium heat; add the dumplings and lightly fry until golden on all sides, about 3 minutes.
Serve hot, optionally with a drizzle of sour cream or a sprinkle of fresh dill.
